title: Smoky Roasted Venezuelan Chicken Roast
description: A bold, Venezuelan-inspired roast that blends sweet and smoky flavors with dried red chilies for a velvety, savory dish perfect for a kosher-friendly feast.

ingredients.name: Dried guajillo chilies
ingredients.notes: soaked in hot water for 10 min

instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C). Rinse the chicken and pat it dry with paper towels.

Prepare the chili paste: heat the dried guajillo chilies in a dry pan for 1 minute to release aroma, then soak them in hot water for 10 minutes. Drain, puree with lime juice, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt.
Combine roasted corn kernels, roasted cocoa nibs, dried oregano, ground cumin, kosher salt, ground black pepper, and the chili paste to form a thick rub.
Pat the rub onto all surfaces of the chicken, ensuring the cavity is also coated, and tuck the legs under the body for even cooking.
Place the chicken on a rack in a roasting pan and drizzle the remaining olive oil over the top.
Reduce the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C), continue roasting for 60-70 minutes, basting every 20 minutes with pan juices and a few extra teaspoons of chili paste.
During the last 15 minutes, sprinkle a handful of roasted corn kernels and cocoa nibs over the chicken for added crunch.
Use a meat thermometer to check the internal temperature; it should reach 165°F (74°C) in the thickest part of the thigh.
Once done, remove the chicken from the oven, tent it with foil, and let it rest for 10 minutes before carving. Serve with lime wedges and optional sliced avocado if desired.
